issues.title: Height of TextField decreases when using OutlineInputBorder and label
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the provided code example using border type UnderlineInputBorder as enabledBorder. Notice that the height adapts to the height of the SizedBox.
2. Change the border type from UnderlineInputBorder to OutlineInputBorder, and the height will decrease.
3. Note: this only seems to be reproducable if the TextField has a label. When removing the label, the height doesn't change when switching border types.

Only tested on Linux desktop.

### Expected results

When changing the enabledBorder of InputDecoration from UnderlineInputBorder to OutlineInputBorder, I expect the height of the TextField to stay the same (200).

### Actual results

The height decreases when changing from UnderlineInputBorder to OutlineInputBorder.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Scaffold(
      body: Center(
        child: SizedBox(
          width: 300,
          height: 200,
          child: TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              fillColor: Color.fromARGB(255, 142, 163, 118),
              filled: true,
              label: Text('Label'),
              enabledBorder: OutlineInputBorder(
                borderSide: BorderSide(
                  color: Color.fromARGB(255, 79, 92, 64),
                ),
              ),
              contentPadding:
                  EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 20, horizontal: 10),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

issues.title: TextFormField vertical padding on web does not align with iOS/Android
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

The `contentPadding` of the `TextFormField` is rendered differently on web compared to iOS/Android. The issue occurs on newest stable 3.32.0 and 3.29.2 version, simple by using `TextFormField` with `contentPadding` on `decoration` attribute. With the `isDense` attribute on true, the padding grows correctly on iOS/Android as the base field height is 24px by adding top and bottom padding. Starting with zero padding, you can see this is one of the few padding values which renders the same height on both platforms.

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/849db64a-008c-43dd-9e40-9652c6bba8d0)

</details>

But using vertical padding like 1px is rendered on web still like there is zero padding.

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b436cca-117c-483a-9962-e053851ed6fb)

</details>

### Expected results

Both platforms should render the field with the same height and accordingly the same padding.

### Actual results

Different heights, which comes from different content paddings.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
return ColoredBox(
  color: Colors.green,
  child: TextFormField(
    decoration: InputDecoration(
      isDense: true,
      contentPadding: EdgeInsets.all(13),
    ),
  ),
);
```

</details>

issues.title: [Windows] Shift key randomly gets stuck in a "pressed down" state forever when using TextFields
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

The problem is that sometimes, when editing text inside a TextField using the Shift key, the Flutter app will keep the Shift key stuck in a "pressed down" state forever.

It happens quite randomly, but generally what seems to trigger the problem is this: 
1. Focus on a TextField widget and write something
2. Use the Shift key, maybe combined with the arrow keys as well (press Shift and select some text with the arrow keys, or press Shift and click with your mouse somewhere within the TextField, ...). Also try to unfocus the whole application (click on your desktop, other app, etc), then re-focus the Flutter app
3. At some point you will notice that the Shift key is logically stuck in a "pressed down" state forever inside the Flutter app. You cannot deactivate/unpress it, no matter what

This issue seems to be related to these
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/115066
- https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/75675

but it seems the problem is still here in Flutter v3.32.8.

Here are some signs to notice when this problem starts to happen:
- when you edit some text inside a TextField, parts of the text are automatically selected (without you pressing the Shift key) -> you can see this happening in the video
- you cannot scroll vertical scroll views with your mouse wheel anymore because the Shift key is stuck in the down state, so you can only scroll horizontally now
- focus groups will now focus backwards (i.e. when you press the Tab key to move to the next focus, the app will actually focus the previous focusable element) -> you can see this happening in the video

### Expected results

Expected = I can use the Shift key normally

### Actual results

Actual = The Shift key is stuck in a "pressed down" state forever, and you have to restart the app to "fix it", but it will happen again at some point

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

I can't paste the actual code from the video, but the widgets are literally just two TextFields with a border and nothing else.
Any TextField will do.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Container(
          alignment: Alignment.center,
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: const TextField(),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

issues.title: [iOS] Portuguese (Portugal) compound character entry creates extra character
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the sample app in the iPad Air 13-inch emulator with iOS 17.5 with a Portuguese (Portugal) keyboard
2. Press `´` and then `a`

### Expected results

`Á` is inserted

### Actual results

`´á` is inserted

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(
    const MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: TextInputExample(),
      ),
    ),
  );
}

class TextInputExample extends StatelessWidget {
  const TextInputExample({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Center(
      child: Padding(
        padding: EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
        child: TextField(
          autofocus: true,
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

issues.title: iPad VoiceOver Not Announcing Characters During Cursor Navigation in TextField using external keyboard
issues.body:
## Description
When using VoiceOver with an external keyboard on iPad devices, character-by-character navigation using arrow keys in Flutter TextField doesn't trigger audio announcements. While the cursor visually moves correctly through the text, VoiceOver fails to announce the character at the cursor position after each movement.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Connect an external keyboard to an iPad
2. Enable VoiceOver in iOS settings
3. Open a Flutter app with a TextField
4. Enter some text in the TextField
5. While in editing mode, use left/right arrow keys to navigate through the text character by character

## Expected Behavior
- As the cursor moves to each character, VoiceOver should announce the character at the current cursor position
- This behavior works correctly in native iOS applications and in Flutter on iPhone devices

## Actual Behavior
- The cursor visually moves through the text correctly
- VoiceOver remains silent and does not announce the character at the cursor position
- No audio feedback is provided to indicate which character the cursor is currently on

## Device Information
- Device: iPad mini 5
- iOS Version: 14.2
- Flutter Version: 3.30.0-1.0.pre.428 (master channel)

## Additional Context
This issue severely impacts accessibility for visually impaired users who rely on VoiceOver for text editing on iPad. The same functionality works correctly on iPhone devices with the same Flutter application, suggesting this is an iPad-specific issue with how Flutter integrates with VoiceOver on iPad.

## Minimal Reproducible Example
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(
    MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('VoiceOver Navigation Issue')),
        body: const Center(
          child: TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              hintText: 'Type text and navigate with arrow keys',
            ),
            autofocus: true,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    ),
  );
}
```
comments:
I don't think your solution is the optimal one.
The problem I'm describing only occurs on iPads, while it works fine on iPhones.
So, it seems to be a specific issue related to iPads, not a general iOS problem.
Also, it's working correctly on Android.

Thanks for the report. I tried on iPhone 15 device in which it works as expected with external keyboard. Currently I don't have physical ipad to verify this, but I'll keep the issue open for team's tracking.
Yes, this issue only exists on iPad, iPhone is normal.
Needs to figure out whether the keyboard is interacting with the UITextInput or the framework textfield. I suspect it was the latter. A potential fix would be that UITextInput handles the key input.

issues.title: [Autofill] Switch to `SecSharedCredentials` on iOS, and explore how we can expose it in the framework
issues.body:
## Use case

I work on a multi-platform e-commerce product that is available in several countries and has many associated domains. When using `AutofillGroup` as recommended by the [docs](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/AutofillGroup-class.html) to save login/signup credentials for the first time, they get saved in association with the wrong domain. 

This is problematic because it can cause confusion to customers. To illustrate, consider the example below.

- Users from Germany access `company.de` on Safari and register their credentials using the keychain (which correctly associates these credentials with the `.de` domain).
- These users decide to access the product via the Flutter mobile App. When they try to log in on iOS, the keychain correctly identifies that the App has the `.de` associated domain and suggests the previously registered credentials.
- Users accept the suggestion and get their username/password auto-filled. 
- However, when pressing on the submission button, they are asked if they want to save new credentials because the **keychain is trying to store them in the wrong domain** (e.g. `company.ie`). 

_"If I have already saved my credentials for that product/service and nothing has changed, why am I being asked to save them again?"_

## Proposal

We need a way to **set which associated domain we want to use for storing credentials** with password managers and `AutofillGroup`

If a new user accesses the App with the `de_DE` locale, his/her credentials must be saved to the appropriate `company.de` domain instead of the other available options.
comments: CC @LongCatIsLooong
I have a similar issue and would love this feature :D please :)

I looked at https://developer.apple.com/documentation/safariservices/supporting_associated_domains and https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/entitlements/com_apple_developer_associated-domains, there currently doesn't seem to be a way to "bind" an associated domain to a locale on iOS? 

@BelfDev do you happen to know if this is doable in an iOS `UITextField`?

@LongCatIsLooong indeed, there isn't a standard way of binding an associated domain to a locale on iOS because the domain format might vary considerably -- making it virtually impossible for the platform to infer the correct binding. However, we might be able to delegate a custom binding mechanism to the App by combining two characteristics of Apple's API:

> iOS supports Password AutoFill on UITextField, UITextView, and any custom view that adopts the UITextInput protocol. ([source](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/password_autofill/enabling_password_autofill_on_a_text_input_view)).

> The **Security.SecSharedCredentials API** provides functions for storing and requesting shared password-based credentials. ([source](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/shared_web_credentials?language=objc))

So, in theory, we could include in the custom `UITextInput` implementation a method that handled the binding. This method could use [SecAddSharedWebCredential](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/1617986-secaddsharedwebcredential?language=objc) to manually save credentials to the correct associated domain. 

From the code-user perspective on Flutter, one could provide an `AutofillDelegate` to indicate how to link the `locale` to a supported `associated-domain`.

E.g. `de_DE` → `company.de`

### Useful resources
- Apple App site association validator tool. Click [here](https://branch.io/resources/aasa-validator/).
- Apple search validator tool. Click [here](https://search.developer.apple.com/appsearch-validation-tool).
- An example code snippet with `SecRequestSharedWebCredential` use. Click [here](https://github.com/mluedke2/ultra-motivator/blob/e8a7f845c504f64a46fe67433994fc63c92f2c01/ios/UltraMotivator/UltraMotivator/SafariKeychainManager.swift#L13-L38).

Repurposing this issue to:
- Change the iOS autofill save implementation to use `SecSharedCredentials` 
- Explore how we can expose the API in the framework (since it's platform specific), or make the platform side extensible.

issues.title: [flutter_svg][web] SVG in TextField prefixIcon/suffixIcon blocks input when semantics are enabled
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

#### Description

On Flutter Web, when using an SVG image (via `SvgPicture.asset`) in the `prefixIcon` or `suffixIcon` of a `TextField`, the text field does not accept any input when accessibility semantics are enabled. The text field appears focusable: the caret is visible, and the field gains focus as expected. However, when the user types, no text appears in the field.

#### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a `TextField` with either a `prefixIcon` or `suffixIcon` containing an `SvgPicture`.
2. Enable semantics with `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()`.
3. Attempt to focus and type into the field.

flutter_svg version: 2.1.0

#### Workarounds

- Adding a `semanticsLabel` to the `SvgPicture` constructor resolves the issue.
- Wrapping the `TextField` in a `MergeSemantics` widget also resolves the problem.

However, these workarounds shouldn't be necessary. The `TextField` should work as expected with `SvgPicture` and semantics enabled, without extra configuration.


### Expected results

The TextField should accept input normally, even when an SvgPicture is used in the prefix/suffix and semantics are enabled.

### Actual results

The `TextField` becomes unresponsive when an SVG is used and semantics are enabled.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/semantics.dart';
import 'package:flutter_svg/svg.dart';

void main() {
  WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();
  SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics();

  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: TextField(
          decoration: InputDecoration(
            prefixIcon: SvgPicture.asset(
              'assets/flutter_logo.svg',
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );

}
```

issues.title: [Proposal][Desktop] Let "ESC" key de-select currently selected item
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Use case

Users expect de-selection to work like it does in other programs.

Selected items (including selected text, widgets, and tab-focused items) all require the mouse to de-select, which is inconvenient. Other desktop programs typically allow de-selecting items with the <kbd>ESC</kbd> key.

## Proposal

Selected items should be de-selected when a user presses the <kbd>ESC</kbd> key. This includes:

- Selected text
- Selected (long-pressed) widgets: see screenshot below
- Tab-focused buttons

![pic](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44756978/145481517-d22dc67e-2086-4f68-b6a4-1c6f12b1984b.png)

I had previously filed a [ticket for Fluffychat](https://gitlab.com/famedly/fluffychat/-/issues/684) and was directed to file it here.

I observed the lack of support for <kbd>ESC</kbd> deselection on the Linux desktop on Wayland, but I think it should be universal.
comments: Interesting proposal. CC @gspencergoog

@Seirdy Could you provide a code sample to reproduce this?

If the selection has caused the focus to change and we can receive the escape key, then I think this could be a straightforward fix by firing something like a DismissIntent.

On Fri, Feb 11, 2022 at 10:42:57AM -0800, Justin McCandless wrote:
***@***.*** Could you provide a code sample to reproduce this?

I was simply referred to this bugtracker from the Fluffychat bugtracker; 
I'm not a Flutter dev. Maybe KrilleFear could share; I'll ping him in 
the Fluffychat Matrix room.

>If the selection has caused the focus to change and we can receive the escape key, then I think this could be a straightforward fix by firing something like a DismissIntent.

I think that any sort of selection or focus, from tab-selection, 
text-selection, long-tap selection (IDK what the technical term is but 
that's what's in the screenshot), to being in a text box, should be 
cancelled with ESC.

If I'm in a text box but I press ESC then tab, I shouldn't type a tab; I 
should change focus. If I'm using keyboard nav with tab-selection and 
arrow-key navigation, pressing ESC should cancel it. Otherwise, I have 
to use the mouse to quit keyboard navigation, which kind of defeats the 
point of keyboard navigation.

Essentially this could be considered a meta-issue for grouping together
all forms of deselection that ESC should handle.

So aside from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98163 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/84416 what else is missing this? It would be helpful to aggregate places where this doesn't work as expected.

I'm slightly concerned about how far exactly this should go since not everything is consistent on desktop. Would it be fair to expect ESC to de-select selected text? Should it unfocus a focused text field if no text is selected? I'm not sure what the general case is for these but playing around with the browser and my IDE there are conflicting approaches. It's certainly doable by just going through a few checks in EditableTextState.

Also am I missing something or does pressing tab in EditableText just always navigate instead of entering anything?

Currently tab always moves focus per [these shortcuts](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/d34376fa40f672acee8ee8cee38b29e584a7765b/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/app.dart#L1160-L1161) in WidgetsApp.  If you remove/override them, then a tab character will be inserted.

For escape behavior, I think ideally we should match the platform's behavior everywhere we can, with Chrome being the standard for web (I don't think I've seen a case of Flutter changing behavior per-browser).  On something like Mac, if there seems to be no consensus for the behavior among apps, probably the standard would be to create a plain text field in a native Mac app in Xcode and see what it does by default.

That may be splitting hairs in some cases but I think that's what we should aim for.

This could also be considered an accessibility issue, as failing to de-select an item would violate all three levels of WCAG 2.2's Guideline 2.1 (Keyboard Accessible). One of Flutter's selling points is accessibility; this makes anything that disqualifies it from the WCAG's most lenient conformance level a big deal.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

issues.title: [Firefox] Composing down to a single character from multiple characters does not replace the composing text until a new event fires.
issues.body:
Composing region on web for firefox using delta model. This issue is very specific and a little hard to pin down.

If the amount of text inputted during composing is more than one character, and it composes down to one character, the text inputting does not replace until the next keypress. Here is a list of "triagable" cases:

- Type 'ni' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This will _not_ replace properly, as it is more than one character (ni.length == 2) and composes down into a single symbol.
- Type 'n' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This _will_ replace properly, as it is one character composing down into one character.
- Type 'ni ho' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This _will_ replace properly, as it is more than one character composing down into more than one character.


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the delta model sample [found here ](https://github.com/flutter/samples/tree/main/simplistic_editor)
2. Activate the Chinese composing keyboard "Pinyin - simplified" on Mac
3. Run the above examples

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Compose the characters "ni -> 霓" see that text replacement works properly, and the entirely of "ni" is replaced with the composed symbol. 

**Actual results:**

'ni' is not replaced with '霓' until a new event is fired after the composition - see that 'ni' stays in the text field along with it's composing output, 霓: something like 'ni霓'. Then when you type a new character such as n, you get '霓n'.

no MVP for this, since setting a delta keyboard up is pretty involved. It is better to just use the Delta Model example linked above and [here](https://github.com/flutter/samples/tree/main/simplistic_editor).

Examples:

working case: 
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48811365/171703331-59953541-5c50-4352-9394-aea94a582370.mov
See that 'n' composes to one character and fully replaces. The same is true for 'ni ho', which composes down into two characters (maybe it is another bug that 'ho' is not being displayed onto the text field) and also replaces fine.

not working case:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48811365/171703788-ebb65486-61d5-4e04-b187-cfb29bed41cb.mov
See that 'ni' is not replaced by it's composed text '你‘ until a new character is placed. The same is true for longer composing text like 'song' which composes down into one symbol.
comments:
CC: @mdebbar @Renzo-Olivares 

Hopefully this issue gets fixed by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105243.

This issue is likely a symptom of whatever is causing [this failing test](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/33590/files#:~:text=test(%27should%20emit%20changed%20composition%27%2C%20()%20async%20%7B) because of events firing in a different order than expected on Firefox for composition. Once the refactor is complete, this should be investigated to see if this problem still exists and if that test keeps failing.

Log format: - newEditingState, as a result of text-input - lastEditingState, the last editing state before the most recent text-input occurred. - Encoded editingDeltaState sent to framework. These are some of the logs I pulled from typing `ni hao ma`. Chrome on the left, Firefox on the right. Looks like when the text is committed on Chrome, the composing region adjusts to length of the composed text, and then another update is sent to null the composing region. On Firefox, the composing region is nulled within the same message that sends the final committed text to the framework. On the `TextEditingDelta` side of things because the composing region is nulled with the final composing text, the text is inserted at the extent of the previous composing range, resulting in `ni hao ma你好吗`.

issues.title: SelectionListenerNotifier selection.range incorrect when selecting over WidgetSpans
comments:
@jaboyc Thanks for the report. Seeing the same behaviour with latest SDK versions.

```
stable : 3.38.4
master : 3.40.0-1.0.pre-55
```

From my investigation the root cause of this issue is that `_SelectableTextContainerDelegate._calculateLocalRange` when aggregating the endOffset, does not consider that the current selectable `selection.NormalizedStartOffset` during iteration may not be where the selection last left off, especially in the case of a non-selectable widget span inside of rich text. This non-selectable widget span will not have a selectable/or content range for itself, and therefore the selection will be discontinuous. This issue also seems to only affect selecting text backwards, selecting forward seems to produce a reasonable range.

I think when solving this issue there are a few questions we should keep in mind:
* What should a user expect out of a discontinuous selection, for example rich text with non-selectable widget spans inside of it.
	- Should it try to calculate a reasonable selection that covers the entire area including non-selectable widget spans?
	- Should there be some discontinuous representation?

I'm not actively working on this but hope this information is helpful. Also happy to review any pull requests fixing this.

comments: (@PixelToast `platform-catalina` is for thing that are specific just to Catalina, which is rare. `platform-mac` is for macOS issues in general.)

The title doesn't match what you're showing in the screenshot. From the `ToolbarOptions` documentation:
> Toolbar is a context menu that will show up when user right click or long press the EditableText.

You're showing the application-level menu, which is not a context menu. The fact that the standard edit menu items don't auto enable and disable is a bug, but not one that related to `ToolbarOptions`. Retitling.

The expected behavior here is that when the FlutterView is the first responder, we enable menu items based on the text/selection state from Flutter. We'll need to do async updates when the state should change so that we can respond synchronously to enable checks.

@leocirto I think the additional buttons should now be present on Android. I tried this on the latest `master` commit. The "Select All" behavior you described I can reproduce, and it should probably have its own dedicated issue. When these additional buttons are added we should keep in mind that on the web adding buttons that depend on platform APIs will not be possible unless the web provides some variant of those APIs. This includes lookUp, searchWeb, and share. This is mainly important on mobile web which is currently using a flutter rendered context menu rather than a native browser menu like on Desktop.

issues.title: Gmail-style Email Input Field in Flutter
issues.body:
### Use case

I am looking for a robust text input widget in Flutter that mimics the behavior of the "To" field in Gmail or similar email clients. This type of input allows users to type email addresses, which then transform into "chips"  upon completion ( pressing Enter, Space, or a comma, or selecting from an auto-completion list). Users should also be able to easily remove these chips by taping backspace once.
 
I have attempted to use existing packages, specifically "chip_input_text_field" (https://pub.dev/packages/chip_inputs_textfield), but encountered a significant limitation. When the cursor is positioned at the very end of a created chip, and the user presses the backspace key, the expected behavior is for the chip to be removed or, in some implementations, for the chip to revert to editable text. However, in "chip_input_text_field", pressing backspace at the end of a chip causes the chip's content to be converted back into a regular string within the text field, rather than deleting the chip itself. This leads to a poor user experience and requires manual re-deletion of the text.
 
This limitation appears to stem from the package's underlying implementation, which uses "WidgetSpan" to replace the text with a chip based on input validation. In this approach, converting a string with 'n' characters into a "WidgetSpan" is treated as a single item. Consequently, the package often replaces the original string with 'n-1' blank spaces, making it difficult or impossible to achieve the desired backspace functionality for chip removal.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a23e3af-7a84-42f5-a2a9-b499a65b8175

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ebc88f8f-9514-4a12-81d6-a1f9ac87f4bb


### Proposal

Instead of treating the "WidgetSpan" as a single character, it should internally be considered to have a length 'n' (corresponding to the original text it replaced). The "TextSelection" mechanism should be designed to handle this 'n' length internally, possibly with an additional boolean flag or property to enable this specific behavior for chip-like use cases.
comments: thanks for the detailed explanation. this looks like a limitation in the chip_inputs_textfield package, not flutter itself. it likely comes from how WidgetSpan and backspace are handled internally. I'd suggest reporting it to the package maintainer or exploring a custom solution with EditableText or TextInputFormatter. closing from here. thanks!

Thanks for the quick response. I understand why this might seem like  a package-level limitation, but I'd like to explain why this is a deeper issue with Flutter's core text handling, not just a problem with one specific package.

Imagine Flutter's text input as a line of individual letters. When you type an email and it turns into a "chip" (or some other widget), the way Flutter's system currently works, it sees that whole chip as a single character not as the representation of the block of text which is converted as chip.

So, when you press backspace, Flutter's system only tries to delete that single character form the actual input, instead of understanding that it should delete the entire chip (which represents a whole word or email).

Because of this, packages that try to create these "chip" inputs have to do a lot of complicated tricks to make it work. They try to "guess" when you're trying to delete a whole chip and manually remove it. 

If Flutter's own basic text tools were smarter about understanding that these chips are like "blocks" of text, it would be much easier for any developer to build a smooth and reliable "chip" input field
@tirth-patel-nc – I think we could reopen this, given the description from @Sureshkanna03
thanks @kevmoo and @Sureshkanna03 for the report. marking it as a new proposal.
#56285 might be related

@Sureshkanna03 are you aware of [this sample](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/examples/api/lib/material/input_chip/input_chip.1.dart) that shows how to have text field chips that are deletable? Is that enough for your scenario?

Provided sample deals with widget span insertion from predefined list. In user email case, all valid emails required to be converted to chips while typing with keypad or physical keyboard.

We are required to use widget span for ui to match chips. But widget spans are not tied to text and text deletion does not delete the span. For reference, we propose the span to be set to text indexes like here.
https://developer.android.com/reference/android/text/style/ImageSpan

On gmail android app, chip is highlighted on pressing backspace key and deleted on pressing the backspace key again. Require customisability to achieve such use cases.

issues.title: TextField should have a selectAllOnFocus
issues.body: `TextField` or `TextEditingController` should have a `selectAllOnFocus` property, just like Android has, to easily make the whole text selected once the `TextField` gets in focus.
comments:
It seems @feinstein is referring to Android's `TextView.setSelectAllOnFocus(boolean)` [method](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/widget/TextView#setSelectAllOnFocus(boolean)). 

Here is a code sample for Android that can be used in `activity_main.xml`
```xml

<TextView
    android:id="@+id/heading"
    android:layout_width="wrap_content"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content"
    android:text="Hello World!"

    android:textIsSelectable="true"
    android:selectAllOnFocus="true"

    app:layout_constraintBottom_toBottomOf="parent"
    app:layout_constraintEnd_toEndOf="parent"
    app:layout_constraintStart_toStartOf="parent"
    app:layout_constraintTop_toTopOf="parent" />
```

cc: @justinmc

Thanks @shivamag00 for the thorough investigation here! After seeing this all written out, I think I lean towards taking no action here, and recommending the approach given above (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/46737#issuecomment-2599715357). It's pretty straightforward despite not being quite as simple as Android's selectAllOnFocus. It also avoids a possible conflict between the way we would implement selectAllOnFocus and the user modifying the TextEditingValue themselves.

@feinstein I'm interested in if you still have any thoughts on this after all this time.

Hi @justinmc, honestly I didn't even remember about this, I didn't miss it much.

The solution should be straightforward, but I think it's expected to be baked in text fields? It could remove the frustration of having to write the boilerplate for this on every project that needs it. But I would put it on a very very low priority, and wouldn't mind if this was closed.

There is currently no spell check (red squiggles) available on flutter for desktop.

## Proposal

-->

Add spell checking to EditableText on desktop platforms.

comments:
Thanks for creating an issue for this!  It's true we haven't yet written any support for spell check on desktop platforms.

CC @camsim99.  Awhile back did you look into what it would take to support desktop platforms?

> CC @camsim99. Awhile back did you look into what it would take to support desktop platforms?

Not in depth, but I think the main thing to look into is which spell checkers to plug into on the engine side.

@justinmc do you know how spell checkers hook into the IME? Do they plug into that behind the scenes and let the IME report issues? Or is spellcheck external to the IME, and applied after an IME reports some bit of text entry?
I hope this is added, Spellcheck is an essential feature for Desktop Flutter apps.

> @justinmc do you know how spell checkers hook into the IME? Do they plug into that behind the scenes and let the IME report issues? Or is spellcheck external to the IME, and applied after an IME reports some bit of text entry?

The IME is doing the detection of spelling errors and generating suggestions.  See https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/30858.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

As far as spell checking libraries to look into for desktop I think for MacOS and Windows you'd just go with the platform spell checkers -- NSSpellChecker and ISpellChecker respectively.

Linux is a little more complicated because it's hard to say what spell checking libraries will be installed by the distribution. I believe that since flutter uses GTK3 you will most likely have access to [libenchant](https://github.com/rrthomas/enchant) on most (probably all) distros, which is a wrapper library around the many other spell checking libraries that might be installed (hunspell, aspell, nuspell). This library is used by gnome's text editor applications and some other gnome applications through either gspell (gtk3) or libspelling (gtk4). It is also small enough (.so is 166kb un-compressed) to be bundled, or statically linked into the flutter runtime for linux if availability is a concern. Apparently it also works for apple's spell checker on macos, so maybe a chance for some code re-use there if it bundled into the app on mac.

I'd be willing to take a stab at implementing at least the linux backend, but it would be cool if someone from the flutter team could approve the libraries before hand.

FYI - we've created a plugin for Super Editor that integrates with Mac spelling and grammar check: https://github.com/superlistapp/super_editor/tree/main/super_editor_spellcheck

We haven't released it to Pub yet because we're waiting to see whether Flutter implements this directly for all platforms.

My recommendation here, as with all other text manipulation, is to fully expose each platform's unique APIs. That's the direction we're headed. If we need to implement Windows and Linux, we'll create separate APIs for those based on those platform APIs.

issues.title: `SelectionArea` supported gestures should have feature parity with `TextSelectionGestureDetector`
issues.body:
`SelectionArea` should have the same supported gestures as `TextSelectionGestureDetector`. This is a pre-requisite for migrating `TextField` from `TextSelectionGestureDetector` to `SelectionArea`. Some of these gestures are also needed for re-implementing `SelectableText` with `SelectionArea` https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104547.

**Platform Common:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| Double tap to select a word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/149295 ✅ | Both |
| Double tap + drag to select word-by-word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/149295 ✅ | Both |
| Double click to select a word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/124817 ✅ | Both |
| Double click + drag to select word-by-word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/124817 ✅ | Both |
| Triple tap to select a paragraph | Implementation is there but needs to be enabled. Static text behavior on native platforms have this disabled. | Both |
| Triple tap + drag to select paragraph-by-paragraph | Implementation is there but needs to be enabled. Static text behavior on native platforms have this disabled. | Both |
| Triple tap to select all in a single-line input field |  | isEditable |
| Triple click to select a paragraph | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/144563 ✅ | Both |
| Triple click + drag to select paragraph-by-paragraph | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/144563 ✅ | Both |
| Triple click to select all in a single-line input field |  | isEditable |
| Long press to select word-by-word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132518 ✅  | Both |
| Shift + click to extend selection to clicked position | #129583 ✅  | Both |

**Platform Mobile:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| On single tap up moves the cursor to the tapped position |  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132682 ✅ | Both |

**Platform Desktop:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| On tap down moves the cursor to the tapped position | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132682 ✅  | Both |

**Platform Android:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| Drag to move cursor |  | isEditable |

**Platform iOS:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| Select word edge on tap up | | Both |
| Drag that begins on cursor moves the cursor | | isEditable |
| Long press to move cursor | | isEditable |
| Single tap on previous selection should toggle toolbar | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/13285  ✅ | Both |

**Platform Linux:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| Triple tap to select a line | #153514 | Both |
| Triple tap + drag to select line-by-line | #153514 | Both |

Other details:
On a windows touch screen device the context menu does not show up until the long press ends in editable/static text contexts. On a long press drag it collapses the cursor at the position when the long press ends (editable text). On a long press hold it selects the word on drag start as well as popping up the selection handles (static text). On a long press drag nothing happens (the word at the long press start is selected) (static text).

Normal touch drag does nothing on static text. Consecutive taps also does nothing on static text / consecutive tap + drag.

On a normal touch drag it acts as a mouse drag (editable text). On Editable text consecutive tap / consecutive tap + drag works.

on tap down/tap up works similar to android for editable text, the selection is set on tap up.
comments: Given that there are so many unimplemented gestures on SelectionArea, is work better spent allowing it to share this code with EditableText instead of reimplementing these?  Or is it not so simple?
Talked offline with @justinmc and agreed to keep a look out for opportunities where we can re-use logic from `EditableText`.
With iOS, Is it possible to combine gestures with SelectionArea, such as long press to select a word, tap to select a sentence/paragraph etc.

issues.title: Unexpected backspace behavior via IME with composing character
issues.body:
There's an unexpected behavior when pressing `backspace` after a character that's being composed.

Steps:

1. Place a caret in a text field or any other IME text entry widget
2. Press `opt+u` to create a composing character: "¨"
3. Press `backspace`

Expected behavior: the composing character should be gone, the text should be empty: ""

Actual behavior: the composing character stays. It takes a 2nd backspace to delete it: "¨"

The expected behavior can be seen here in a GitHub text entry.

issues.title: Themeable TextField font/style
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Use case

A generic Flutter theme, such as [Yaru](https://pub.dev/packages/yaru), should be able to specify a thinner font for text input fields than button-like widgets, such as dropdowns and list tiles.

- The current stable (3.3.10) uses `TextTheme.subtitle1` for `TextField`.
- The current master (3.7.0-16.0.pre.22) uses `TextTheme.bodyLarge` (M3) and `TextTheme.titleMedium` (M2).

The problem is that adjusting these commonly use text styles affects many other widgets, not only text input fields.

<details><summary>sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MaterialApp(
    theme: ThemeData(
      useMaterial3: true,
      inputDecorationTheme: const InputDecorationTheme(
        isDense: true,
        border: OutlineInputBorder(),
      ),
      textTheme: TextTheme(
        subtitle1: const TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w300), // stable
        bodyLarge: const TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w300), // master (M3)
        //titleMedium: const TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w300), // master (M2)
      ),
    ),
    home: Scaffold(
      body: Padding(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.all(24.0),
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.spaceEvenly,
          children: [
            TextFormField(
              initialValue: 'TextField',
            ),
            DropdownButtonFormField(
              items: const [
                DropdownMenuItem(child: Text('Dropdown')),
              ],
              onChanged: (_) {},
            ),
            const ListTile(
              title: Text('ListTile'),
              subtitle: Text('Subtitle'),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    ),
  ));
}
```
</details>

| Default | `TextTheme.subtitle1` (stable) | `TextTheme.bodyLarge` (master, M3) |
|---|---|---|
| ![Screenshot from 2023-01-10 20-24-33](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/140617/211643294-2c9a9441-8ff3-43f0-a2d5-58f5fa2e5cc2.png) | ![Screenshot from 2023-01-10 20-24-47](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/140617/211643307-98f635d0-6ad0-4a6b-9948-5420335e61fe.png) | ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/140617/211643865-56d53636-7298-477f-97e0-19af8881033d.png) |

## Proposal

Either introduce `TextFieldTheme` or add a new entry to `TextTheme` to allow changing `TextField`'s font without affecting button-like widgets.

In my opinion, this would be a uniform, clear solution that would require relatively little effort.

issues.title: Allow to specify floatingLabelGap
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I want a tighter gap (smaller) between a floating label on a TextField, and the content of the TextField. Right now it's hard coded. But it doesn't have to be.

Consider this situation: figma on the left, emulator on the right:

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/5642485/31b36756-793d-4385-a4cc-9f90e5eae83d)

See how the "Username" label is much closer to the "SomeGuy" user name in the figma? By about 4 pixels. There is no way for me to duplicate that aspect of the design (short of recreating the entire TextField-InputDecorator functionality myself).

for more details see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76622954/override-inputdecorator-widget-functionality-internally-called

### Proposal

Very simply: parameterize the 4.0 hard coded number. I've tested this out on my local machine, works great, solves the problem, and we can use the InputDecoration class to parameterize it. Submitting a pull request soon.
comments:
Thanks for the report @lastmeta 
Can you take a look at [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/110425) similar issue and check if it resembles your case ?
Also check [this](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67632090/how-to-add-gap-between-label-text-and-user-input-in-textformfield) SO link and see if it helps to resolve your issue or not.

Moreover, see if [this](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/FloatingLabelBehavior.html) property is helpful or not.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. In reverse order: 

> Moreover, see if [this](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/FloatingLabelBehavior.html) property is helpful or not.

I'm aware of that property, it's not helpful at all in my case. (I don't want to 'turn the label on or off' I want to move it to a different location).

> Also check [this](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67632090/how-to-add-gap-between-label-text-and-user-input-in-textformfield) SO link and see if it helps to resolve your issue or not.

I saw that one when I originally searching. I don't want to add to the gap, I want to subtract from the gap.

> Thanks for the report @lastmeta Can you take a look at [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/110425) similar issue and check if it resembles your case ?  

This issue, I hadn't seen yet. It is very similar to my case, almost exactly the same.

However, the author of that issue wants more than I want. He wants more control over label placement, as I do, but he's also asking for more control over other aspects of the border and background, and placement of the text. 

I found ways to manage those other elements with a combination of managing the InputDecoration class (as is ideal) and kind of manually producing the background where I want it. (He could do what I did too if he was more piecemeal about it, but he wants a whole solution).

The only thing we don't have control over, is moving the label closer to the text content of the field. That's hardcoded. 

So my purposed solution is to simply parameterize that hard coded number and I'll handle the rest of the design myself, as maybe he should also. He's asking for a rewrite of functionality (which could turn out better) but I'm just asking for small addition (which would solve the hardest thing to solve in his problem as well).
Thanks for the update and feedback.

> Thanks for the update and feedback.

No problem, I also added more details to the [other issue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/110425) if you want to see my piecemeal solution in code.

If anyone else is experiencing this, please let us know by giving a 👍 reaction to the issue description above and commenting on the PR https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130039 if it fixes your problem.

I looked at the current implementation, ` floatingLabelHeight = (4.0 + _kFinalLabelSize * labelStyle.fontSize!) * MediaQuery.textScalerOf(context).textScaleFactor;` seems to be an estimate of the height of the floating label when fully contracted. But I don't know where the `4.0` comes from. 

I think it's already possible to move the floating label down by using the `InputDecorator.label` API with an additional `Transform`  widget.

issues.title: Autocomplete<T> missing features desktop and web users expect
issues.body:
## Current 2.10.2 Behavior
1. With the Autocomplete options displayed clicking the option with the mouse selects the option, and focus is placed in Autocomplete text box with the cursor at the end of the text.  _PERFECT BEHAVIOR_.

2. With the Autocomplete options displayed using the up and down arrow keys different options are displayed.  Then pressing ENTER on an option, selects that option.  BUT, focus is not visible.  The user needs to press the TAB key once to get focus on the text box. _PROBLEM_

The two above behaviors should be the same, but they are not.  Can this be fixed so that when the ENTER key is pressed the Widget behaves the same as being clicked?

3.  If the user enters text in the Autocomplete and presses ENTER or TAB, the text is accepted but the onSelected callback is not invoked. I understand why this behaves like this but at a minimum, a text entry callback should be added so that developers would be advised when the user entered text without selecting an option.


## Use case, three new behaviors needed

**ENTER and TAB keys:**  Flutter Desktop and Web users expect that the ENTER and TAB keys behave the same when pressed over an Autocomplete option.

When the options are visible, it is natural to use the up and down arrow keys for option navigation and then for a user to press either the TAB or ENTER key to select the option.

Desktop users expect and need to be able to run a desktop or web line of business application with the keyboard when performing data entry functions.  

**Validator:** No Validator function is available for the Autocomplete widget.  Desktop and web line of business applications require field validation on all fields.  This is missing for Autocomplete.  Even a super simple required validator needs to be implemented.  NOTE:  The below gist demonstrates how to provide a user-provided `fieldViewBuilder` that the user can add any TextFormField properties they desire.

**Text Entry Option:**  Currently, Autocomplete allows text entry but does not invoke the onSelected callback.  At a minimum, provide another callback so developers can handle text entry as well as item selection that is invoked when the ENTER or TAB key is pressed.

Workaround:  For now, I copied the SDK Autocomplete and RawAutocomplete widgets and modified them to support the above requirements for my line of business app.

## Proposal

1. Make the ENTER and TAB keys behave exactly as a mouse tap does when an option is clicked.
2. Add a Validator argument to Autocomplete or tell developers to provide `fieldViewBuilder` to the Autocomplete widget.
3. Add a callback for text entry made, this can be invoked when the user presses ENTER or TAB.

## Solution and Demo of Desired Behavior.

This 3 file gist provides a README.md, a customized Autocomplete widget, a small Flutter app demonstrating using the customized Autocomplete app.

[https://gist.github.com/longtimedeveloper/0b718244117f78d337489d9227d32538](https://gist.github.com/longtimedeveloper/0b718244117f78d337489d9227d32538)

issues.title: TextInputConnection should let objects listen for IME connection closure
issues.body:
An IME connection is established with these pieces: `TextInput`, a `TextInputConnection`, and a `TextInputClient`.

```dart
final myConnection = TextInput.attach(myInputClient, myConfig);
```

`TextInputConnection` has a `bool` that reports `isAttached`, but there's no way to listen for a change to this attachment.

The `TextInputClient` has a method called `connectionClosed`, which, I assume, represents a notification that the IME connection has closed. However, policies about when to open and close a connection are usually independent from the policies that apply text input messages from the platform. Therefore, the use of `TextInputConnection` should be able to exist independently from the code that implements `TextInputClient`, and this requires that `TextInputConnection` receive a notification when its connection is closed.

```
Flutter 3.7.0-1.2.pre • channel beta • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision c29b09b878 (9 days ago) • 2022-12-19 18:30:12 -0600
Engine • revision 8c99b0feaf
Tools • Dart 2.19.0 (build 2.19.0-444.2.beta) • DevTools 2.20.0
```
comments: CC @justinmc @Renzo-Olivares

This concern seems valid to me.

Looking at the current code, it does seem a little convoluted to call connectionClosed on TextInputConnection's TextInputClient instance and then immediately call back to the TextInputConnection.

Would it work for your use case if it went TextInput -> TextInputConnection -> TextInputClient instead?  That way TextInput would inform TextInputConnection that it's being closed without needing to rely on the implementation of TextInputClient's connectionClosed method.

### How it works now

 1. TextInput receives the platform channel message `TextInputClient.onConnectionClosed` and calls `TextInputClient.connectionClosed`.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b7881e5b64e28a84a66ab30b29d35484428ea062/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L1899-L1900

 2. If using EditableText, then `TextInputConnection.connectionCloseReceived` is called.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b7881e5b64e28a84a66ab30b29d35484428ea062/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3095-L3097

### Proposed changes

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/118283

> Would it work for your use case if it went TextInput -> TextInputConnection -> TextInputClient instead

Yes, I think so. The most important part is that whatever object holds the `TextInputConnection` shouldn't need to also hold the `TextInputClient` and vis-a-versa. The job of the connection is independent from the job of applying deltas, and both of those jobs might want to know when the connection closes.

issues.title: [Windows] External voice dictation tools (e.g. Typeless, Windows Voice Typing) cannot detect Flutter TextFields
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Install an external voice dictation tool (tested with [Typeless](https://typeless.com/) and a Whisper-based tool).
2. Run a Flutter desktop app with a standard `TextField`.
3. Focus the `TextField`, activate dictation, and speak.
4. The tool **cannot detect the TextField** and fails to inject text.

### Expected results

The dictation tool should detect the focused `TextField` via Windows UI Automation (UIA) and inject text directly — as it does with native Win32, WPF, UWP, Electron, and other desktop frameworks.

### Actual results

The dictation tool **cannot locate any editable text control** in the Flutter window via UIA.

- **Typeless** falls back to a "Copy last transcription" popup — it found the window but no injectable text field.
- **Whisper-based tools** similarly fail to inject into the focused field.

Windows built-in Voice Typing (`Win+H`) and Voice Access likely have the same problem, since they rely on the same UIA / TSF infrastructure.


### Root cause analysis

Two main gaps were identified:

**1. UIA text-editing patterns not properly exposed**

External tools find text fields by querying UIA for `IValueProvider` (`SetValue()`) or `ITextProvider`. Flutter's `AXPlatformNodeWin` (from Chromium) implements `IRawElementProviderFragment` and some UIA patterns, but `FlutterPlatformNodeDelegate` doesn't seem to correctly expose them for `TextField` widgets. The `ITextProvider` / `StringSearch` implementation is also known incomplete (#117013).

**2. No TSF — still on legacy IMM32**

Flutter's Windows embedder uses **IMM32** for IME support (`text_input_manager.cc`), not **TSF (Text Services Framework)**. TSF is how modern Windows routes speech recognition, handwriting, and keyboard input via `ITextStoreACP`. Without a TSF text store, any input going through TSF (including `Win+H`) has nowhere to write. Microsoft has [stated](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/develop/input/input-method-editor-requirements) that IMM32 is being deprecated.

Additionally, when a dictation tool shows an overlay, it can steal focus from the Flutter window — and Flutter's text input pipeline depends on window focus to receive `WM_*` messages, so even clipboard-based fallback injection may break.

### Related issues

- #117013 — Incomplete `StringSearch` for `AXPlatformNodeTextRangeProviderWin`
- #181313 — IME activated when no TextField has focus
- #36057 / #176403 — Touch/virtual keyboard issues in tablet mode (same underlying infrastructure)

### Suggested fix

1. **Fix UIA pattern exposure** — Make `FlutterPlatformNodeDelegate` properly expose `IValueProvider` (with working `SetValue`) and `ITextEditProvider` for text fields. This alone would fix tools like Typeless that inject via UIA.

2. **Migrate to TSF** — Implement `ITextStoreACP` to replace IMM32-based text input. This would fix `Win+H`, Fluid Dictation, and all TSF-based input methods, and is also the proper fix for the touch keyboard issues (#36057 / #176403).

### Current workaround

As an app-level workaround, a **clipboard bridge** works: monitor `WM_CLIPBOARDUPDATE` and auto-insert clipboard content into the focused `TextField` when updated by an external process. This covers tools like Typeless that fall back to clipboard, but it's a hack — not a substitute for proper UIA/TSF support.

### Environment

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.38.3, on Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19045.6466], locale zh-CN)
    • Flutter version 3.38.3 on channel stable
    • Framework revision 19074d12f7 (2025-11-20)
    • Engine revision 13e658725d
    • Dart version 3.10.1
    • DevTools version 2.51.1

[✓] Windows Version (10 Pro 64-bit, 22H2, 2009)

[✓] Visual Studio - develop Windows apps (Visual Studio Community 2022 17.14.16)
    • Windows 10 SDK version 10.0.26100.0
```

issues.title: TextField maxLines should be a double, not an int.
issues.body:
We need to be able to define a fractional maximum number of lines for a TextField. When using an integer number of lines, and there is a lot of text, the user will only see a few of the lines and may think that there are no more.

On the contrary, if you define, say 5.5 lines, and there are in fact, say, 10 lines, the user immediately notices that there is more text to the bottom. In other words, half-lines are visual cues to the users that they are able to scroll to see the rest. 

It's a **small change** that **improves usability**. To be consistent, I would make minLines also double, although in terms of usability that one is probably not important.

This is NOT a breaking change, and preserves existing maxLines behavior. 

Note: I'm the author of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/19105 which was fixed by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/27205
comments: @justinmc Justin, I was expecting this would make into your PR, but since it did not and the original issue was closed, I'm opening this new issue. I'd ask you to please consider including this small change if you happen to touch that code again. Thanks.

One way to implement this change would be to make `maxLines` a `num`, i.e. allow it to either be set as an `int` or `double`. (Would be similar to #147976, and unlike the repeated calculations in a `Flex`, I don't think it'd noticeably impact performance.)
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

3. Will this change also be applicable to `Text` widget and `TextPainter`?

The `maxLines` value in `TextPainter` is not used to calculate the height of text. So I am assuming the change in `TextPainter` and `Text` should not be required. Is my understanding correct?

issues.title: [web] Button becomes un-tappable in Flutter Web when a TextField is present.
comments:
The TL;DR of the issue is that the customer's `index.html` was lacking a `<!DOCTYPE html>` declaration.

Without it, Chrome was rendering in ["Quirks" mode](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Quirks_Mode_and_Standards_Mode), causing some of our elements (the forms we use to implement text editing) to have a _slightly different_ user agent stylesheet than in Standards mode.

In particular, **forms have an unexpected <sub><sup>(to me)</sup></sub> `margin-block-end: 1em` CSS that is not present in standards mode.**

This extra margin caused the `flt-text-editing-host` (which is supposed to not have a size) to grow by 16px every time a new form was added to the DOM.

We need to:

1. Reset the `margin` CSS for the `form` elements, so text editing doesn't break in quirks mode.
2. Let developers know when they forget to set the doctype on their index.html file, so they can correct it!
3. Investigate why forms are piling up in the `flt-text-editing-host`, and clear them once the text editing disconnects?

There is no way to set the width of a TextFormField without setting the width of the error text underneath it.

Problem is, if you ever update TextFormField's code, I'll have to be in synced.

Add a "width" property to TextFormField. It's possible to make it backwards compatible in case it's null.

issues.title: Gaining/losing focus and opening/closing the keyboard must not be strongly coupled
issues.body:
As of now, gaining/losing focus and opening/closing the keyboard are strongly coupled, and they shouldn't be. Please see: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863. 

One good solution is a change in `FocusNode`. It could have a few more methods besides `unfocus` (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/7247). to maintain backwards compatibility I suggest:

-  `closeKeyboard()` which would close the keyboard without removing focus from the TextField. 
-  `openKeyboard()` which would open the keyboard and connect it to the textField even if the TextField previously had focus already (please note `focus` doesn't open the keyboard if the keyboard already has focus). 
- `shouldOpenKeyboard` is a mutable field which is `true` by default. When the `TextField` gains focus by the user tapping it, or by the calling of `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(focusNode);` it would check this field to decide if it should open the keyboard or not.

issues.title: TextInputType.emailAddress should imply autoCorrect: false
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Create a textfield like this:

```dart
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress
)
```

Run this on iOS. (I didn't check on Android yet.)

You get something like this: ` _______ `

Start typing an email address beginning with a well-known name.

` jim_______ `

Type ".", and notice that the name gets capitalized.

` Jim.______ `

For me, this was unexpected.

To get the behaviour I expected, that is an email address wouldn't autocorrect words, I had to additionally and explicitly do:

```dart
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress,
  autocorrect: false
)
```

I don't think it ever makes sense to autoCorrect on an email field. So, I'm proposing that setting `keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress` should imply setting `autocorrect: false` unless `autocorrect` is explicitly set to `true`.

issues.title: [iOS] Tapping selection to toggle the toolbar doesn't work when using system context menu

> [@hellohuanlin[](https://teams.googleplex.com/huanlin@google.com)](https://github.com/hellohuanlin) Any ideas for how to solve this based on the native context menu APIs? Like if we could prevent Flutter from receiving taps that close the context menu that would work... I did some logging, and got this sequence: (1) Engine receives onDismiss callback (2) Framework receives onDismiss event relayed from engine (3) Framework requests engine to present menu (4) Engine presents the menu (5) Engine receives onPresent callback An easy workaround can be: 1. Keep a timestamp of the most recent `onDismiss` callback 2. When needing to present a menu, check this timestamp first. If it's within a certain threshold (say 0.1 second), do not present it. We can put this logic either in framework or the engine. I'm leaning towards putting in the framework, to keep our engine dumb (i.e. it simply follows commands by the framework).

issues.title: Triple tap gestures in a TextField are blocked by selection handles overlay in some situations
issues.body:
# Problem: 
On certain platforms like iOS, when the selection handles overlay pop up on a `double-tap`, a third tap might be prevented from being detected by the `TextSelectionGestureDetector` if the consecutive tap occurs directly where the selection handles might appear.

## Flow of a triple-tap not on a word edge:
When double tapping a word, the word is selected. When this happens the selection handles overlay is shown. This Overlay contains a GestureDetector that handles dragging and tapping on the selection handles.

If a triple tap is attempted, the same sequence occurs and the word is selected and the selection handle overlay pops up on the second tap. On the third tap the paragraph is selected.

## Triple-tap at a word edge issue:
If a triple tap is attempted at the edge of a word, where the selection handles overlay pop up on a double tap, they may block the TextSelectionGestureDetector below it from receiving the third tap. The TextSelectionGestureDetector will not receive any PointerEvents for that third tap, and therefore does not participate in the GestureArena, so the third tap is never detected.

The selection handles overlay block hit testing from further propagating to widgets directly below it because `_RenderTheatre` used by `Overlay` stops hit testing after it hits the first child.
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/f4caee6efbc0b0094f3cee9e31a7486e3d030819/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/overlay.dart#L842-L854

## Proposed Solutions:
1. Use `IgnorePointer` and its `ignoring` parameter to conditionally make the selection handles overlay invisible to hit testing to allow the third tap to propagate to the `TextSelectionGestureDetector` below it. On a double-tap down, `ignoring` would be set to enabled and when the consecutive tap tracker resets (this means there is no possibility of having further consecutive taps) we disable `ignoring`.
- Introduces a new API in `EditableText` to toggle the selection handles ignoring flag.
- Introduces a new API in `SelectionOverlay` to rebuild selection handles.
- Introduces a new API in `SelectionOverlay` and `TextSelectionOverlay` to set the selection handles ignoring flag.

2. Use a new widget in the selection handle overlay that allows the handles to hit test themselves, and allow hit testing to propagate to the widgets below it.
- Widget is private, no new API introduced.
- `panSlop` of `PanGestureRecognizer` used in selection handles must be reduced so it declares acceptance faster or else there is a noticeable delay in the drag of the selection handles.

3. Use the new widget from solution 2, except add a `passThrough` parameter to conditionally allow the selection handles overlay to hit test itself and pass through hit testing to the widgets below it. On a double-tap down, `passThrough` would be set to enabled and when the consecutive tap tracker resets (this means there is no possibility of having further consecutive taps) we disable `passThrough`.
- Introduces a new API in `EditableText` to toggle the selection handles pass through flag.
- Introduces a new API in `SelectionOverlay` to rebuild selection handles.
- Introduces a new API in `SelectionOverlay` and `TextSelectionOverlay` to set the selection handles pass through flag.
comments:

issues.title: Add debug tooling for focus and IME connections
issues.body:
As I'm working with user interfaces that have more complex focus and IME interactions, I'm finding it difficult to track associated changes.

I think it would be helpful to be able to visualize the focus path, from root, to the widget with primary focus. For example, all widgets in the focus path might be given a border and a tiny number in the corner that shows the tree distance from the root. The widget with primary focus could have a special color.

Also, I'd like to be able to track changes to the IME connection. For example, is an IME connection open right now? Ideally, I'd also like to know which widget owns that IME connection. That later part is more difficult, because there's no requirement that a widget own the IME connection. So maybe that requires an opt-in API. Nonetheless, when debugging issues at moments where one widget's IME connection closes and another is supposed to open, it would be helpful to have some quick toggle-able visual indicators through Flutter Inspector.

**EDIT:**
Here are a couple other ideas as I'm working through focus issues.
 * Log why each focus change happens. Did a node request "unfocus"? Did another node request "focus"? Focus changed in some way that I didn't want it to, but it's unclear how to root cause what happened, because anything in the entire app could be involved with the change.
 * Tap to select a widget that handles gestures. Flutter already has a "tap to select widget" mode in the inspector. But what about when I'm looking for the widget that handles gestures at a particular (x,y)? With things like `Stack`s, it's easy to end up with invisible or translucent widgets that sit on top of the widget that handles a gesture. This makes it difficult to locate which widget is actually handling a tap, for example.

It's not as nice as having a visual representation on top of the widgets, but it logs the data.
